articles.title: Economist Ashok Lahiri from Bengal to become Niti Aayog vice-chairman, Gobardhan Das named as member
articles.description: Economist Ashok Lahiri and scientist Gobardhan Das from West Bengal are set to join Niti Aayog. Lahiri, a former chief economic advisor, will be the vice-chairman. Das, director of IISER Bhopal, will serve as a member. This appointment brings distinguished expertise to the policy think tank. Their contributions are expected to shape future economic and scientific initiatives.

articles.title: Ashok Lahiri to be NITI Aayog vice-chairman, Gobardhan Das member: Report
articles.description: Lahiri is a former chief economic advisor to the government and has served as a member of the 15th Finance Commission

articles.title: When the acquisition looks great but the footnote doesn't: How Indian managements turn deals into balance-sheet fiction
articles.description: Acquisitions are among corporate finance’s most celebrated events. But the harder story usually emerges later, when the accounting begins to reflect what the deal actually delivered. This is not always about outright fraud. It is often about judgement. Used carefully, that judgement can reflect business reality. Used aggressively, it can make an ordinary deal look exceptional, a weak business appear stable, and a balance sheet seem stronger than it really is.

articles.title: Promoters Buying: 9 Nifty 500 stocks that saw stake hikes in March quarter
articles.description: Promoters are showing increased confidence in several Nifty 500 companies. Their shareholding has risen in the March 2026 quarter compared to the December 2025 quarter. This trend suggests optimism about future company performance. Investors may find this a positive indicator. Companies like Godrej Properties and Adani Energy Solutions are among those with increased promoter stakes.

articles.title: US stocks: Nuclear reactor maker X-Energy valued at $11.9 billion in stellar Nasdaq debut
articles.description: X-Energy's Nasdaq debut saw shares surge 30.9%, valuing the Amazon-backed nuclear reactor developer at $11.9 billion. The company raised over $1 billion in its IPO, aiming to deploy its small modular reactors to power AI infrastructure and meet carbon-free energy goals. This marks a significant capital milestone for X-Energy's ambitious plans.

articles.title: GST fault line: Supreme Court to rule if taxman can bundle years into one sweeping show-cause notice
articles.description: The Supreme Court is set to decide if tax authorities can issue a single show-cause notice for multiple financial years. This procedural issue impacts how tax exposure is assessed and litigated, with potential to aggregate liabilities and blur limitation boundaries. The court will determine if the CGST Act permits composite proceedings across years or mandates year-by-year liability initiation.

articles.title: The Average Household Is a Millionaire With a $1.3M Net Worth — But Why Do People Still Feel So Broke?
articles.description: A funny thing has happened on paper. The average U.S. household is now sitting on millionaire-level wealth, yet the average grocery run still stings and the ...

articles.description: Karaikal installs shade nets at key locations to provide relief from summer heat for commuters and pedestrians.

articles.description: A lower ratio can indicate untapped investment potential, especially in frontier markets where economic growth may not yet be fully reflected in stock valuations.

articles.description: Besides the bottom-line, M&M Financial's total income went up 13.3% to Rs 4,810.07 crore from Rs 4,245.09 crore in the corresponding quarter of last year.

articles.title: China Pushes Yuan Global As Dollar Dominance Faces Challenge
articles.description: China is actively promoting the renminbi as a global currency to lessen its dependence on the US dollar, viewing this shift as crucial for national security.

articles.title: Actis picks Standard Chartered to kick off $2 billion sale of BluPine
articles.description: Actis is selling its Indian renewable energy firm BluPine Energy. The company has a capacity of 4 gigawatts. Standard Chartered Bank is advising on the sale. The price tag is $2 billion. This follows Actis's previous successful sales of renewable energy platforms in India. Deal activity in the sector remains steady as investors seek exits.

articles.title: Russia Explodes At Europe's €90 Bn 'Loan' For Zelensky; 'Enjoy Getting Played Again'
articles.description: Dmitry Medvedev has ignited a fresh controversy by blasting the European Union’s €90 billion loan package to Ukraine, claiming the burden will ultimately fall on ordinary Europeans while Brussels “fantasises” that Russia will foot the bill. In a fiery post on X, Medvedev mocked EU leaders and accused them of draining taxpayers’ pockets to bankroll Kyiv’s war effort with little hope of repayment.

articles.title: India growth expected to be around 6.5% this year: Gita Gopinath
articles.description: India's economy is projected to grow at 6.5% this year. Global risks persist, impacting supply chains and potentially leading to food price increases. Companies are absorbing costs, but this may not last. Central banks might delay rate cuts due to muted global demand. Any oil shock will affect many sectors.

articles.title: Ashoka Buildcon secures power sector related project in Angola
articles.description: Ashoka Buildcon announced that the company had submitted bid to the Ministry of Energy and Water, the Republic of Angola, for the project viz. 'Electricity Sector Improvement and Access Project'.

articles.title: Barometers trade with deep cuts; Nifty trades below 23,900
articles.description: The Nifty traded below the 23,900 level. All the sectoral indices on the NSE were traded in the red, with IT, media and pharma shares declining the most.

articles.title: Pani puri vendor sells cart for wife's hospital bill, customer invests Rs 1.5 lakh for fresh start
articles.description: A pani puri vendor sold his cart to cover his wife's hospital bills, leaving him with nothing. One loyal customer tracked him down, skipped the sympathy, and handed him a Rs 1.5 lakh business plan.

articles.title: 'May not be sufficient': FM Sitharaman sounds alarm on AI threats to Indian banks
articles.description: Finance Minister flags that decades of strong cybersecurity may be insufficient against AI-driven threats, directs banks to work under an IBA-led mechanism to identify vulnerabilities and invest in new defences.

articles.description: The shift is​ being accelerated by the increased market volatility and flat returns, making it increasingly difficult for time-constrained, high-income investors to actively manage their own wealth.​ ​The promise is to generate higher returns than traditional index or actively managed mutual funds by ensuring the seamless integration of advice and execution

articles.title: Economic growth, prosperity remain primary impulse for changes in India: India's Ambassador to the US Vinay Mohan Kwatra
articles.description: India's Ambassador to the US, Vinay Mohan Kwatra, highlighted economic growth as India's primary driver for change, expressing concern over a global "control regime" trend. He detailed India's "7 per cent-plus GDP growth" alongside governance reforms and infrastructure development, including digital advancements, as key transformations.

articles.title: Real estate deals down 63% to $763 million in Jan-Mar against Dec quarter: Report
articles.description: India's real estate sector saw a significant 63 percent drop in deal value during January-March 2026. This decline to USD 763 million occurred because of fewer large-ticket transactions. The period recorded strong deal volumes with 32 transactions, but the overall value was impacted. Private equity deals and merger and acquisition deals also saw a decrease.

articles.title: BMC to discuss renaming KEM hospital today
articles.description: The proposal, put forward by Mumbai suburban district’s joint guardian minister Mangal Prabhat Lodha, seeks to rename the city’s largest civic-run hospital as “Kaushalyashreshta Eklavya Memorial Hospital”

articles.title: Struggling Nike will cut around 1,400 jobs in efficiency push
articles.description: Nike job cuts: Sportswear giant to lay off around 1,400 employees globally, mainly in North America and Europe, as it battles falling sales, rising competition, China slowdown and shifts focus to core sports and supply chain efficiency

articles.title: India’s Goldilocks moment is over, macro situation will worsen
articles.description: The fiscal burden of the West Asia crisis on the Centre could be around 0.5 per cent of GDP. The uncertain environment will also make it difficult for the Centre to meet its disinvestment target

articles.title: RBI a net buyer of US dollars in February
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India became a net buyer of $7.4 billion in February, a shift from January's net sales. This likely benefited from a stronger rupee after the India-US trade deal announcement. The real effective exchange rate (REER) for the rupee decreased in March, indicating undervaluation and supporting export competitiveness.

articles.title: Cisco shows switch that can connect quantum computers of different kinds
articles.description: SAN FRANCISCO, April 23 : Cisco Systems on Thursday showed a switching chip that it says will be able to connect quantum computers of different types, another step in its effort to eventually connect an internet of quantum machines the way its gear connects the existing internet.Like other major tech firms su

articles.title: SP Group to raise Rs 25,400 crore via bonds, loans
articles.description: Shapoorji Pallonji Group is nearing the closure of a ₹25,400 crore debt raise by May 15, featuring annual coupons around 18.75%. This fundraising, part of Project Ascent, will utilize USD bonds and bespoke loans to refinance existing debt, including bonds secured against its Tata Sons stake.

articles.title: Panic buying, Nepal price gap fuel demand surge in UP border districts
articles.description: Officials said the spike in border districts is partly driven by petrol being taken across to Nepal, where prices are around ₹60 per litre higher. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation amid concerns of diversion, even as rumours of a post-election fuel price hike have added to public anxiety. Social media speculation around rising global crude oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, despite stable domestic rates, has further fuelled panic, though officials insist no price revision has been announced.

articles.title: Yuan to top Yen in currency options trading: LCH
articles.description: The yuan is poised to surpass the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in foreign-exchange options, according to LCH. This shift, expected by 2028, reflects growing yuan internationalization and market demand for currency diversification beyond the dollar.

articles.title: These large-, mid- and small-cap stocks can give more than 22% return in 1 year, according to analysts
articles.description: When a market is in a secular correction due to fears that the economy might take a hit for some reason, the fall is generally across the board. The question at this point of time: Is the Indian market at the beginning of a second phase of such a correction? The answer is not as clear as it should be, because of several reasons.

articles.title: Strike vote at major Australian LNG plant set to close
articles.description: PERTH/TOKYO, April 24 : A vote on whether workers strike at Inpex’s Ichthys liquefied natural gas facility in Australia over pay and conditions will close later on Friday, after an employment agreement offered by the Japanese gas giant was refused last week. A strike at the 9.3 million metric-ton-a-year fa
